name: astrophage-eval-set-v1
description: Named eval set for the Astrophage multi-agent PR pipeline. Tests scoring/parsing logic without running the full agent stack.

cases.description: parseVerdict correctly extracts an ACCEPT decision from a clean reviewer response

input.agentOutput:
I reviewed the PR diff carefully. The change correctly reads credentials from an environment variable and exits with a clear error when it is missing. No hardcoded secrets detected.
{"decision":"accept","reason":"Change reads credentials from environment variable, exits cleanly on missing auth, no plaintext token exchange","nonNegotiable":false}

cases.description: parseVerdict correctly detects a NON-NEGOTIABLE rejection when a hardcoded secret is present

input.agentOutput:
Found a hardcoded API key on line 42. This violates the non-negotiable constitution rule.
{"decision":"reject","reason":"Hardcoded API key found: client_id='AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE'","violatedRule":"No hardcoded secrets, API keys, or client_ids in source code","nonNegotiable":true}

cases.description: parseVerdict extracts a negotiable REJECT (e.g. naming convention) and sets nonNegotiable to false

input.agentOutput:
The code looks functionally correct but the env var is named MY_APP_TOKEN instead of the project convention MY_APP__TOKEN.
{"decision":"reject","reason":"Environment variable should use double-underscore convention: MY_APP__TOKEN","violatedRule":"Environment variable naming convention","nonNegotiable":false}

cases.description: parseVerdict handles JSON wrapped in markdown code fences (common LLM output pattern)

input.agentOutput:
The change looks great. Here is my verdict:

```json
{"decision":"accept","reason":"All constitution rules satisfied","nonNegotiable":false}
```

cases.description: parseVerdict returns a synthetic reject verdict when the agent response contains no valid JSON — prevents the pipeline from hanging

input.agentOutput: I looked at the code. It seems fine to me. Nothing wrong here.

cases.description: parseTestResult correctly identifies a PASS when the agent outputs 'ok' and ends with PASS

input.agentOutput:
Running tests in /home/ubuntu/bawarchi...
ok  	github.com/chinmayrelkar/bawarchi	0.234s

All tests passed.
PASS

cases.description: parseTestResult correctly identifies a FAIL when go test output contains '--- FAIL'

input.agentOutput:
Running go test ./...
--- FAIL: TestHandleAuth (0.01s)
    auth_test.go:23: expected error when token missing, got nil
FAIL	github.com/chinmayrelkar/bawarchi	0.015s

FAIL

cases.description: parseTestResult treats 'no test files' as a pass — not every package has tests

input.agentOutput:
cd /home/ubuntu/bawarchi && go test ./...
?   	github.com/chinmayrelkar/bawarchi/cmd	[no test files]

PASS

cases.description: Pipeline convergence logic: a pass+accept in round 1 immediately sets status to 'merged'
Pipeline convergence logic: a non-negotiable reject on round 1 sets status to 'unresolved' and stops immediately
Pipeline convergence logic: repeated negotiable rejects exhaust MAX_ROUNDS and result in 'unresolved'
Pipeline convergence logic: a test failure on round 1 followed by pass+accept on round 2 results in 'merged'
